title: scabiosa ochroleuca var. webbiana
intro: Scabiosa ochroleuca var. webbiana, also known as the yellow scabious, is a perennial plant that belongs to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Scabiosa ochroleuca var. webbiana has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant is also said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
